item.title: From plastics to explosives, the oil surge is set to spike prices well beyond the fuel pump
item.description: From mining to agriculture, higher oil prices from the war in the Middle East are not only increasing fuel costs, but prices for key chemicals as well. Can businesses cope?

item.title: Trump calls out Australia in blistering post declaring the US does not 'need' ally support in Iran
item.description: The United States president has declared America does "not need the help of anyone" in the war he launched against Iran, days after he called on allies to help reclaim a key shipping route.

item.title: Senegal stripped of AFCON title, Morocco declared winner months after controversial final
item.description: Senegal's 1-0 win in the Africa Cup of Nations final has been overturned by an appeals board and a 3-0 victory awarded to Morocco after Senegalese players left the pitch in protest during the controversial January clash.

item.title: Forget fireworks, Bali's new year is marked with silence, fasting, meditation
item.description: Instead of fireworks and countdowns, Bali rings in its new year in complete silence.

item.title: Fuel supply chain coordinator to be proposed at national cabinet meeting
item.description: The prime minister will convene a virtual gathering of state and territory leaders tomorrow, with the talks to focus on strengthening state-federal cooperation in the face of demand-driven shortages on fuel.

item.title: Farmers and conservationists form 'emergency alliance' against invasive species
item.description: An unconventional union of environment and farming groups has been announced with a plan to take on a common enemy — invasive pests.

item.title: Silverton gardener's 'little oasis' featured in Gardening Australia magazine
item.description: At Helen Murray's home in Silverton in far west New South Wales, where temperatures often push past 30 degrees Celsius in summer, figuring out what will grow is often a jigsaw puzzle of trial and error.

item.title: John Travolta's old luxury jet bound for Australian aviation museum by ship
item.description: The long-awaited fuselage and wings of John Travolta's Boeing 707, which he is donating to a regional NSW aircraft museum, have been loaded onto a ship in the US, bound for Australia.

item.title: Aboriginal rangers meet on NSW South Coast to merge past and present
item.description: The first Aboriginal rangers conference for the NSW South Coast focused on how to best take care of country by combining cultural knowledge from elders with new technology.

item.title: Fuel costs from Iran war forcing Australians to cancel domestic road travel, flights, cruises
item.description: Domestic travel is also taking a hit as a result of rising fuel costs across the globe, with regional communities likely to feel the most impact.

item.title: Is antibacterial or regular hand soap best for killing germs?
item.description: Experts say there is an array of options when it comes to hand soaps, and some labels can be misleading.

item.title: Australian facilities damaged in Iranian attack on Middle East air base
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has revealed that an Iranian projectile hit a road just outside the Al Minhad Air Base, but no Australians were injured.

item.title: Oil tankers have never stopped, industry assures, though they are in uncharted waters
item.description: Ships carrying oil are continuing to come to Australia as expected and should do so for the next month, and the industry is urging that while the picture beyond that is uncertain and complicated, "the tankers have never stopped coming".

item.title: Liberals defend SA election flyer that confused Palestinian flag with another
item.description: The South Australian Liberals have defended an election campaign flyer that attacks a Labor MP for his views on the Middle East and carries the slogan "Defend Australian Values", but appears to mistakenly show an image of a Sudanese flag instead of a Palestinian one.

item.title: Narelle is expected to become a category five cyclone — what does that mean?
item.description: Cyclone Narelle is expected to intensify into a category five cyclone — find out what that means.

item.title: Labour Hire Authority links worker exploitation in the Yarra Valley to organised crime
item.description: The Labour Hire Authority has accused an apple grower and labour-hire business of exploiting workers in the Yarra Valley, as concerns emerge about organised crime links.

item.title: NSW Police investigating DJ Haram for potential racial hate law breaches at Biennale of Sydney
item.description: In a video uploaded to social media, Zubeyda Muzeyyen, known as DJ Haram, can be heard urging attendees to "oppose the Zio-Australian-Epstein empire" before she led the crowd with a chant.

item.title: Cape York residents urged to make a plan to shelter from Cyclone Narelle
item.description: There are "places of refuge" but no designated cyclone shelters on Cape York Peninsula. Local leaders say residents need to make a plan to stay safe as a potential category four or five storm approaches the Queensland coast.

item.title: Bulldogs challenge Bronson Xerri to make case for NRL return, Dophins ready for Sharks fans' abuse
item.description: Cameron Ciraldo speaks for the first time since Bronson Xerri's discontent at the Bulldogs emerged over the weekend.

item.title: Donald Trump, Xi Jinping and the meeting looming over Middle East war
item.description: The US president said last month that his planned visit to China on March 31 would be "a wild one". A lot has changed since that confident declaration and most of it has weakened his position.

item.title: Daly River evacuee's heartbreak at seeing community flooded for second time this year
item.description: Flood evacuees from Daly River in the remote Northern Territory — who have now spent more than a month this year in evacuation centres — say many had lost "everything they worked for".

item.title: Hawks warned not to underestimate Swans as AFL ladder leaders lose Isaac Heeney to injury
item.description: The Sydney Swans will be understrength after losing another key player to injury ahead of facing Hawthorn at the MCG tomorrow night.

item.title: Call for tougher anti-vilification penalties as hate flyers target Perth LGBTQIA+ community
item.description: Police are investigating flyers dropped in mailboxes across Perth targeting members of the LGBTQIA+ community.

item.title: Possum found in Hobart Airport gift shop soft toy section
item.description: A real possum nestled among soft toys and animals in a gift shop has thrilled passengers — but caused "pandemonium" after going on a sightseeing tour of the departure lounge.

item.title: Pauline Hanson in confrontations with SA premier and MP as One Nation poll support continues
item.description: South Australia's premier tells Pauline Hanson he will work with any One Nation MPs elected on Saturday, as a new poll shows the right-wing party remains ahead of the Liberals.

item.title: Wednesday finance with Daniel Ziffer
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Japan storm into Asian Cup final against Matildas with ominous dismantling of South Korea
item.description: Japan has produced a frighteningly good display to overpower South Korea 4-1, booking a spot in the Women's Asian Cup final against the Matildas.

item.title: ACT Brumbies veteran James Slipper set to break Super Rugby's record for most games played
item.description: The prop will have played more Super Rugby than anyone else and is hoping to continue next season.

item.title: What is a healthy birth weight? Urgency to close the gap for all babies
item.description: The Productivity Commission's latest Closing the Gap data shows just four out of 19 targets are on track. Advocates call for urgent funding to address low birth weights.

item.title: Australia to toughen tobacco crime laws as cigarette and vape black markets surge
item.description: Criminal gangs cashing in on Australia's illicit tobacco boom will face tougher penalties and expanded police surveillance powers in a fresh crackdown on the black market.

item.title: Military officers arrested over acid attack on human rights campaigner Andrie Yunus in Jakarta
item.description: Four Indonesian officers have been arrested and detained over an acid attack on human rights campaigner Andrie Yunus in Jakarta.

item.title: Iranian women's football team back in Iran, state media announces
item.description: The delegation of players and staff included five people who initially accepted Australia's offer of asylum but later changed their minds.

item.title: Treasury can't predict how Trump ends this war, with or without allies
item.description: Even the best minds at Treasury can't know what will happen with global oil supplies, or how Donald Trump plans to end this war, with or without the help of allies.

item.title: Heart surgeries in Tasmania reduced from daily to two a week amid indefinite industrial action
item.description: Daily heart surgeries have been cut to two a week in Tasmania while a small group of crucial specialists strikes for better pay and conditions. But negotiations with the government have halted, and locums are being sought to plug resulting healthcare gaps.

item.title: Put your Oscars knowledge to the test with our 2026 Academy Awards quiz
item.description: Awards season might be over for another year but the memories will live on in our minds … and in the questions of this Academy Awards quiz.

item.title: Ryan Gosling is humanity's last hope in the new space epic Project Hail Mary
item.description: Project Hail Mary hits Australian cinemas this week, joining Oscar-nominated animation Arco, off-the-wall slasher Dolly and BAFTA-winning biopic I Swear.

item.title: Pressure mounts on government to release cabinet documents sooner after motion passes parliament
item.description: Tasmania's minority parliament has delivered the Liberal government a stinging verdict on integrity and transparency, with two motions passed without the government's support.

item.title: Steve Irwin made totemic crocodile Old Faithful famous. Recently laws may have been broken in its capture
item.description: Missing: One 80-year-old, 4.5 metre totemic crocodile. Last known whereabouts: Australia Zoo. Welcome to the fight for Old Faithful.

item.title: Why don't Australian banks offer 30-year fixed-rate home loans? Borrowers would benefit from them
item.description: A new report says Australian borrowers would benefit from 30-year fixed-rate home loans, and they should be made available.

item.title: Price gouging at petrol stations may not be illegal, experts warn, as PM considers supply chain tsar
item.description: Drivers fed up with massive price spikes at the bowser are being warned not to expect a wave of fuel company prosecutions over allegations of price gouging.

item.title: Could a fracture in conservative politics cost right-wing parties a seat in SA's parliament?
item.description: Labor's popularity in South Australia is set to lead to a large number of seats for the party at the upcoming state election, as various right-wing parties compete for a red seat in the upper house, writes ABC Chief Elections and Data Analyst Casey Briggs.

item.title: Some South Australians will vote in two elections this weekend
item.description: Thousands of First Nations South Australians will be eligible to vote in two elections on Saturday, in a potential historic first for the state.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Matildas on notice as Japan soar into final — here's what we learnt from the second semifinal
item.description: Ultimate control, goals from everywhere and an eccentric coach from Greenland. This is what we learned from Japan's supremely impressive semifinal victory over South Korea.

item.title: Koala habitat logged by farmers caught unaware of new environment laws
item.description: Koala habitat has been bulldozed in an apparent breach of new environment laws that took effect in December, with fears farmers are unaware of their new obligations.

item.title: Checking online for fuel prices? Here's what to keep in mind
item.description: Petrol Spy, a national website that displays petrol, diesel and LPG prices across the country, says page views have gone up six to eight times since the Iran conflict started.

item.title: Can ARN Media financially survive a legal battle with Kyle Sandilands and Jackie 'O' Henderson?
item.description: Even prior to this month's colossal blow-up of the Kyle and Jackie O Show, ARN Media's future had already been teetering on the edge.

item.title: No 'definitive time frame' to end Iran war, Pentagon to seek funds from Congress — as it happened
item.description: US Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth says the US will end the war in Iran quickly, because the US President Donald Trump "knows better" than to keep it going for a long time.

item.title: Brisbane's 'third places' providing a spot away from work, home, and online echo chambers
item.description: In an increasingly digital world, Brisbane residents are still heading out to enjoy third places to take a break, enjoy life, and expose themselves to new ideas.

item.title: Cyclone Narelle tracker map: Where is it forecast to cross Queensland?
item.description: Track the path of Tropical Cyclone Narelle and see live wind speeds as the system crosses Far North Queensland.

item.title: The secret bush sport that's been hidden away for three decades
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Cost-of-living crisis widens regional-metro higher education gap
item.description: Pursuing higher education is difficult for regional students who uproot their lives, with advocates calling for support to level the playing field.

item.title: Wind gusts reach 315km/h as category five Cyclone Narelle nears Qld coast — as it happened
item.description: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle has intensified into a category five system and is expected to cross the coast on Friday morning. Read back on how events on Thursday unfolded.

item.title: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle to cross Qld coast as category five system
item.description: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle is expected to be a category five system when it crosses the coast, producing winds near its eye of 315 kilometres per hour.

item.title: Police investigate deaths of kangaroos and joey killed in Lake Macquarie hit-and-run
item.description: Police are investigating the deaths of kangaroos on suburban streets in Lake Macquarie in what has been described as a "deliberate" hit-and-run attack.

item.title: Senegal calls for corruption investigation after Africa Cup of Nations Nations title stripped
item.description: The Senegalese Football Federation plans to appeal the decision that gave its Africa Cup of Nations title to Morocco, with Senegal's government calling for an investigation into alleged corruption of African football's governing body.

item.title: BOM declares cyclone watch for NT communities with Tropical Cyclone Narelle set to hit on Saturday
item.description: Parts of the Northern Territory have been issued a cyclone watch with Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle expected to make landfall on Groote Eylandt from Saturday as a category three system.

item.title: Galatasaray player Noa Lang suffers freak hand injury, Barcelona knocks out Newcastle out of UEFA Champions League
item.description: Galatasaray forward Noa Lang suffered a freak hand injury when he caught his thumb on an advertising hoarding in his team's 4-0 loss to Liverpool in the Champions League.

item.title: Why did the RBA raise interest rates? Will higher petrol prices have same effect?
item.description: This week's rate rise left ABC readers asking "how would hiking rates stop inflation caused by an unavoidable cost like petrol that's spiking due to a war on the other side of the world?"

item.title: AFL news wrap: Matt Rowell set to see physio on broken finger, Hawks prepare to reach 'top notch' against Swans
item.description: Gold Coast has started its 2026 campaign well, even while last year's Brownlow medallist continues to be sidelined due to a broken finger.

item.title: Dance performance takes on expectations of disability, demanding the right to take risks
item.description: Challenging assumptions about what disabled people can and can't do is at the centre of this performance at the Joondalup Festival.

item.title: NRL news: Dragons forward Loko Pasifiki Tonga cleared of serious injury after being hospitalised
item.description: A Dragons training session ends with a player stretchered off and assisted from the ground into an ambulance before being rushed to hospital.

item.title: ACCC investigating allegations of anti-competitive conduct by major fuel suppliers
item.description: The competition watchdog has announced it is investigating allegations of anti-competitive conduct by major fuel suppliers in regional and rural Australia.

item.title: Cable Beach venue in Western Australia's far north charged with liquor breaches
item.description: A cafe in the Kimberley town that has strict alcohol restrictions has been charged with multiple liquor breaches, while two of its workers have been fined.

item.title: Australia isn't sending a warship to the Persian Gulf. But if the government wanted to, is there one to send?
item.description: Australia has made it clear it's not planning to send a warship to the Strait of Hormuz and naval experts warn our ships would be of minimal help if they were deployed.

item.title: Ship crew involved in French Polynesia cocaine bust sent to immigration detention
item.description: The crew of a vessel that was intercepted carrying nearly 5 tonnes of cocaine has been escorted off the ship by Australian authorities.

item.title: Iranian oil tankers are using fake data to get sanctioned fuel out of the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Changing flags, creating new names, and faking their GPS data, ABC NEWS Verify investigates how Iranian-linked oil tankers continue to export oil during the war.

item.title: Botanist gave early warning on rehab difficulties for troubled Griffin Coal's Collie mine site
item.description: The WA government insists Griffin Coal's obligations have not changed, but changes to its mining agreement could allow the company to walk away from rehabilitating its Collie mine site.

item.title: Albanese unveils new national fuel supply taskforce to steer response to Middle East war
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has named Anthea Harris, a former chief of Australia's energy regulator, as the national coordinator of the taskforce.

item.title: '16 years in the making': Renowned Let Them Theory author Mel Robbins on her success
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Prolonged Middle East war and AI profitability 'downgrades' are major risks for economy, RBA says
item.description: Interest rates, war in the Middle East and AI advancements are expected to hit the Australian economy, but the RBA says we are prepared.

item.title: Israel hits Iran South Pars facilities in world's largest natural gas field
item.description: The Israeli bombing of Iranian facilities in the South Pars gas field and Iran's retaliation targeting energy infrastructure across the Persian Gulf, mark a significant point of escalation in the US-Israeli war with Iran.

item.title: Unemployment rate rises to 4.3 per cent, but rate hike fears persist amid inflation concerns
item.description: Australia's unemployment rate rose to 4.3 per cent in February, up from 4.1 per cent, in seasonally adjusted terms.

item.title: Bundaberg residents call for buyback scheme after three major floods in 16 years
item.description: There are calls for the Queensland government to buy back homes in Bundaberg's flood zones, but the disaster recovery minister says the focus is on mitigation measures like floodgates.

item.title: Iran will not boycott FIFA World Cup, but seeks to get United States-based matches moved
item.description: Iran has no intention of boycotting the FIFA World Cup, but is seeking to have its group stage matches moved out of the United States.

item.title: What Doesn't Kill You | Ghanim Al Shnen
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: French jockey Marina Morel's death subject of inquest
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Steph Catley says Asian Cup final not 'mission impossible' for Matildas despite ominous Japan form
item.description: The Matildas aren't daunted by Japan's ominous form ahead of what shapes up as a blockbuster Asian Cup final.

item.title: Kangaroo Island considers introducing 'last cat policy' in bid to eradicate feral cats
item.description: Kangaroo Island Council is considering banning the introduction of all cats to the island, in a move the mayor is calling the "last cat policy". If successful, the ban would make the South Australian island one of the world's biggest inhabited islands to be free of cats.

item.title: Family who conceived through IVF at Royal North Shore Hospital seeks DNA testing
item.description: A family is pursuing DNA testing after hearing about an IVF mix-up at the same Sydney fertility clinic used to conceive its baby.

item.title: How boosting vaccinations could prevent meningitis outbreak in Australia
item.description: A growing outbreak of meningococcal B and meningitis has killed two young people in the UK as the number of infections climb. Here's why infectious disease professor Paul Griffin thinks Australia should be worried.

item.title: Inside Lizzy Hoo's wild life escapades: music, Mongolia and the moments that shaped her
item.description: Comedian Lizzy Hoo loves an adventure, which is how she ended up at a music festival outside Ulaanbaatar watching her new favourite band.

item.title: Albanese hopeful about end of war against Iran as budget looms
item.description: Perhaps the prime minister was hoping US President Donald Trump was tuning in to Triple M when he claimed that the attack on Iran had now achieved its aims, and offered an off-ramp for the war to end.

item.title: Health insurance premiums to hike as much as 25 per cent, CHOICE analysis shows
item.description: Some Australians with private health insurance will face premium increases of as much as 25 per cent from next month, according to new analysis from CHOICE.

item.title: SA Liberals and Labor differ wildly on how much they say election promises will cost
item.description: Labor says the opposition's promises will cost $2.4 billion more than they say, as both parties target the public sector as a way to save money on the budget ahead of the state election.

item.title: Artist Ben Quilty makes case for culture in a room focused on roads
item.description: One of Australia's most acclaimed artists brought humour and frustration to a council meeting weighing commerce against culture.

item.title: Danish Royals meet and greet in Hobart
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Tony Popovic says young players have 'great opportunity' with Socceroos despite Adrian Segečić's defection to Croatia
item.description: Socceroos coach Tony Popovic maintains young players have an opportunity to represent Australia at the highest level despite losing a younger player to a European rival.

item.title: Residents also have concerns about the 'self-evacuations' from Jilkminggan
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Remote residents concerned about withheld flood relief payments as Cyclone Narelle looms
item.description: Stranded residents of Jilkminggan in the Big Rivers region say they need their full flood relief payments now, after finding out this week they would only get one-quarter of the money while they remain in shelters in Mataranka.

item.title: Great Gunston gets Hawks home at the MCG
item.description: Hawthorn finishes over the top of an understrength Sydney side to knock off the ladder-leaders at the MCG, thanks to a four-goal haul from Jack Gunston.

item.title: Thursday Finance with David Chau
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Dogs hold on to down Canberra in brutal wet-weather battle
item.description: It was far from easy, but Canterbury have held on for a narrow win over the Raiders in torrential conditions in Canberra.

item.title: Report reveals major gaps in WA's response to family and domestic violence
item.description: A report tabled in parliament finds significant gaps in how agencies respond to high-risk family and domestic violence, as a prominent advocate calls for urgent action.

item.title: Iran’s Foreign Ministry spokesperson: “This is a war of self-defence”
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Iranian deputy foreign minister labels US 'terrorists' and issues warning to Australia
item.description: Iranian deputy foreign minister Esmaeil Baghaei accuses the US of "terrorist acts" and issues a warning to Australia about being on the wrong side of history.

item.title: Cyclone Narelle downgraded to category 2 as west coast communities warned to stay in place - as it happened
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle downgraded to a category 2

item.title: China cracks down on illegal fentanyl trade after Trump delays Beijing meeting
item.description: The country is one of the world’s highest producers of the chemicals used to produce the synthetic opioid in America.

item.title: BTS's new comeback album, concert, Netflix documentary: What you need to know
item.description: K-pop supergroup BTS makes a comeback from mandatory military service today with a new album, concert and documentary. Here's why that's a big deal.

item.title: PM denies Australia out of step with allies after joint statement
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese denies Australia is out of step with its allies after several countries, including the UK, France and Germany, released a joint statement on the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: History-making Oscars moments, a royal visit and fans storm the NRL field
item.description: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

item.title: How award-winning pianist Andrea Lam learned to let go of perfection
item.description: She made her debut with Sydney Symphony Orchestra at 13, but don't call Andrea Lam a piano prodigy. The award-winning pianist shares how she's kept kicking career goals without losing her love of the music.

item.title: Rise in women woodchoppers prompts introduction of new female events
item.description: Like her father, grandfather and great grandfather before her, woodchopping is part of Kallie's family legacy.

item.title: Prime minister's department requests modelling for gas and coal tax to shield Australia from impact of war
item.description: The prime minister's department has requested options to impose a new tax on gas giants to help shield Australians from the economic shock of the Middle East war.

item.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle downgraded to a category two
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle has been downgraded as it moves across Cape York Peninsula, but authorities warn it could dump half a metre of rain in Far North Queensland, even as it continues to weaken.

item.title: Migrants say new laws stopping temporary migration from war-torn countries 'a slap in the face'
item.description: Migrants and human rights advocates say new laws stopping temporary migration from designated countries will keep families apart and make Australia's immigration system less predictable and fair.

item.title: How is the Middle East war affecting Australian superannuation funds?
item.description: Experts are urging Australians to stay calm and weather the storm as the effects of the war in the Middle East reverberate through financial markets.

item.title: Jim Chalmers is trying to use the current crisis as a springboard for reform
item.description: If the treasurer is serious about budget repair the government has to avoid the inevitable pressure for more cost-of-living relief which will only grow as many families' budgets become unmanageable.

item.title: We asked you to tell us what matters to you ahead of the SA election. Here's what you said
item.description: We asked you to tell us what matters before you vote at the state election, with the environment and housing ranked among your answers.

item.title: Raiders prop Josh Papali'i facing NRL suspension, glass shatters at Canberra Stadium injuring coach
item.description: Canberra Raiders prop Josh Papali'i is facing a one-match suspension for a high tackle during his side's 14-10 loss to the Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs on Thursday night.

item.title: The Matildas' last Asian Cup final at home shows how far they've come in 20 years
item.description: The last time the Matildas played an Asian Cup final in Australia, there were 5,000 people in the stands at a suburban football ground in Adelaide.

item.title: For the Liberals, the SA election will be both an end and a beginning
item.description: Whatever happens at the SA election, this weekend will mark the end of a term for the SA Liberals marred by leadership changes, defections, public displays of disunity and even criminal charges.

item.title: Lightning Ridge's $37m Opal Centre runs out of money before construction is complete
item.description: The Australian Opal Centre in Lightning Ridge risks becoming a "white elephant" as construction indefinitely stalls and an auditor raises concerns about the organisation's viability.

item.title: We might have just found where Australia's red line is with Donald Trump
item.description: The war in the Middle East is heaping pressure on Australia's relationship with the US and we could be about to see if that comes with consequences.

item.title: Sydney independent service station, taxi drivers hit hard by rising petrol and diesel prices
item.description: The fuel crisis is squeezing independently owned service stations, with some being forced to sell fuel at above-average prices, even though their margins are near zero or below cost.

item.title: One Nation's Cory Bernardi says he will reimburse Gina Rinehart for SA election private plane flights
item.description: The Labor Party has written to South Australia's electoral commissioner asking him to look at One Nation's use of a private plane and whether it is allowable under the Electoral Act.

item.title: FIFA mandates female coaches in its women's competitions ahead of world cups
item.description: Every team in a FIFA women's competition will be required to have at least one female head or assistant coach, part of sweeping new regulations from soccer's governing body aimed at boosting the number of women coaching at the highest levels.

item.title: Maggie Beer brings aged care nutrition project to outback renal health hub
item.description: Culinary icon Maggie Beer and her team are working with an Alice Springs organisation that provides dialysis and aged care on Indigenous homelands.

item.title: Victim-survivor's 'demoralising' efforts to secure safe social housing
item.description: A young woman says she has been left with "severe trauma" after fleeing violence only to months later still be fighting to find stable, suitable social housing.

item.title: Aston Martin F1 dismisses rumours it will replace team principal Adrian Newey
item.description: Struggling Formula 1 outfit Aston Martin says Adrian Newey will remain team principal despite increasing media speculation he will be replaced by Audi's Jonathan Wheatley.

item.title: As it happened -- Major bank says oil price could soar to $US150
item.description: The CBA says there is a "strong likelihood" of the oil price reaching $US120‑150 per barrel.

item.title: Rare Rymer apples found on NSW Southern Tablelands farm
item.description: The Rymer variety of cooking apple, once believed to have died out, has been found on a rural property in the NSW Southern Tablelands.

item.title: AFL investigates after Hawthorn's Nick Watson accuses Sydney fans of throwing drink bottle
item.description: Hawthorn's Nick Watson says Sydney Swans supporters threw drink bottles at him after the Hawks defeated the Swans on Thursday night.

item.title: Calls for urgent action on flood mitigation for Alice Springs
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Urgent calls for flood mitigation in Alice Springs, as insurance premiums skyrocket
item.description: A shock flash flooding event in Alice Springs last month saw more than 100 millimetres of rain fall on the catchment in a matter of hours. The deluge has reignited calls for urgent action on flood mitigation in the outback town, as insurance companies calculate the risk with eye-watering premiums.

item.title: Townsville truckie wants action on fuel as Queensland prices hit record high
item.description: Almost everything arrives in outback Australia by truck. Truckies say record-high fuel prices will have to be passed on.

item.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle set to make landfall as category four system
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Numbulwar evacuated as other NT communities prepare to shelter from Cyclone Narelle
item.description: Ahead of Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle's expected impact on the eastern Top End, about 500 people from one community have been evacuated to Darwin, while others prepare to "shelter in place".

item.title: Fuel and freight woes from Middle East conflict could end some carrot farms in WA, warns industry body
item.description: Shipping disruptions due to conflict in the Middle East mean Western Australian carrot growers have lost access to their main customer.

item.title: What's a library worth? Report puts dollar figure on 'invaluable' institutions
item.description: Every dollar invested in the State Library of NSW is worth $4.25 in community benefits, according to a new report commissioned by the library.

item.title: How to choose sheets and bed linen that last as long as possible
item.description: Shopping for sheets can be overwhelming and expensive. This is what textile experts say to look out for.

item.title: Albanese, Burke shouted at as anger erupts at Eid prayers at mosque
item.description: An event to mark the end of Ramadan was disrupted by a protest against the prime minister and home affairs minister, who were visiting the mosque in Western Sydney.

item.title: Historic flour mill wall in Mount Gambier will be destroyed despite community opposition
item.description: The remnants of a 140-year-old historic flour mill will be demolished to make way for a housing development despite a regional community calling for the city's history to be preserved.

item.title: Intense winds as Cyclone Narelle hits Far North Queensland
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle hits Far North Queensland, with residents in the region taking shelter from intense winds.

item.title: Iran war drives up cost of living in Asia, hits crucial tech industries
item.description: The Middle East war triggers everything from fuel rationing in Sri Lanka to a helium supply crisis for Asia's crucial semiconductor industries.

item.title: White House meeting with Japan's PM turns awkward as Trump jokes about Pearl Harbor
item.description: When he was asked why the US did not notify allies like Japan ahead of the strikes on Iran, Donald Trump made a quip drawing a parallel with the Japanese air raid in 1941.

item.title: Video shows news crew narrowly avoiding Israeli strike in southern Lebanon
item.description: An English-language Russian state broadcaster has accused Israel of deliberately attacking its news crew in southern Lebanon. IDF says it will investigate.

item.title: The ABC is investigating aged care again and we want to hear from you
item.description: We're launching an investigation asking you — staff, insiders, those navigating the aged care system and their supporters — to help the ABC understand if the government's promise of reform has been delivered.

item.title: How the 'four right blokes' turned the ABC's coverage of the VFA into must-watch TV for footy fans
item.description: As Aussie Rules was forced to reckon with a national competition, four men helped make the VFA must-watch TV on the ABC for Victorian footy fans.

item.title: Nathan Vikatos jailed for 28 years for murdering toddler son Alexi
item.description: Nathan Vikatos has been jailed for more than 28 years for what a judge described as the tragic, horrific and untimely death of his three-year-old son, Alexi, in May 2023.

item.title: When girls don't feel comfortable or confident in their uniforms it can cause them to drop out of sport
item.description: Studies in Australia and overseas show a big percentage of girls drop out of sport when they become teenagers. One of the contributing factors is the clothes they have to wear.

item.title: What to watch, read and listen to this weekend: New James Blake and a sun-drenched crime drama
item.description: This weekend go down the rabbit hole with a podcast about a Canadian cult or discover James Blake's latest sonic salve.

item.title: NT cattle station Aroona sells to Argentinian family for $44 million
item.description: An Argentinian family has expanded its portfolio of NT cattle stations with the purchase of Aroona Station.

item.title: Why gas giants have suddenly gone to ground over Iran
item.description: Are Australians about to endure a re-run of the economic pain brought on by the Ukraine war? Or something much worse?

item.title: Teenager Moise Kouame makes history with win over Zachary Svajda at ATP Masters Miami Open
item.description: Teenager Moise Kouame steals the spotlight as Australians Alexei Popyrin and James Duckworth crash out of the multi-million-dollar Miami Open.

item.title: Poverty increased after Victoria 2022 floods while disposable income decreased, report finds
item.description: Poverty and unemployment rose after Victoria's 2022 floods, with those in inundated areas experiencing lower disposable income than those who weren't affected, according to a new report.

item.title: Unmasking Banksy is missing the point of his work. What's so wrong with a little mystery?
item.description: Does Banksy's success mean we are more entitled to demand he pull his mask off, show his face? Does it matter that he says his anonymity is core to his art?

item.title: Ashton Hurn lives in hope of becoming SA's premier after election
item.description: Polls predict the Liberals are heading for a wipe-out in South Australia on Saturday, but party leader Ashton Hurn says she is still working with the goal of becoming the state's leader rather than analysing what went wrong.

item.title: One Nation candidate Aoi Baxter wanted in the UK for failing to attend court on sexual touching charge
item.description: South Australian One Nation candidate Aoi Baxter has a warrant for his arrest in the UK after he failed to show up to court to face a charge of sexually touching a woman without consent.

item.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle leaves trail of damage as residents shelter in boats, shipping containers, WWII bunker
item.description: Far North Queensland residents say Tropical Cyclone Narelle has left a trail of damage across Cape York.

item.title: Trump has postponed his meeting with Xi, but China is not wasting any time on it
item.description: Donald Trump has postponed a meeting with Xi Jinping because he is busy with Iran and the Middle East — and that's good news for China.

item.title: Alana King stakes claim for T20 World Cup recall with dominant performance against West Indies
item.description: Alana King gives Australia's selectors a timely reminder of the quality she possesses, helping secure a win against the West Indies.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Japan coach talks favouritism ahead of final against Matildas
item.description: Japan's coach says determining a favourite for the Women's Asian Cup final between his team and Australia is a matter for bees and flies.

item.title: Leaders make final pitches ahead of SA election, One Nation leading Liberals in latest poll
item.description: The major parties have made their final campaign pitches to South Australian voters as a Newspoll survey puts primary support for One Nation at 22 per cent, ahead of the Liberals on just 16 per cent. One political analyst says it is a "transitional moment in South Australian politics".

item.title: Epworth releases details into hospital review following endometriosis allegations
item.description: A South Australian health commissioner will lead the review of clinical governance at Epworth Hospital, after allegations a top gynaecologist performed unnecessary operations to remove endometriosis.

item.title: Federal government approves 1,695 gas wells in rural Queensland
item.description: A major multinational gas venture has just secured federal approval to ramp up extraction in rural Queensland — locking in operations for the next 55 years.

item.title: Bellamy pinpoints Storm's downfall as Broncos achieve 10-year first
item.description: Storm coach Craig Bellamy identifies the area in which he believes his side let themselves down, as the Broncos celebrate a rare win in Melbourne.

item.title: Katherine residents brace for second major flood in two weeks as Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle approaches NT
item.description: Just two weeks after its worst flood in almost 30 years, Katherine businesses are being sandbagged and a field hospital has been set up as locals brace for another major weather event.

item.title: Coroner finds paramedic failures in death of Phillip Mark Walton in custody
item.description: A coroner has found paramedics failed to meet standards of care for a man who died from cardiac arrhythmia while being restrained by police.

item.title: Perth man Luke Hanif Sekkouah who murdered mother-of-four Erica Hay sentenced to life in prison
item.description: A Perth man who murdered his partner, a mother of four, before setting her on fire has been sentenced to life in prison with a non-parole period of 24 years.

item.title: Josh Giddey sets Australian NBA assists record as Luka Dončić scores 60 to continue Lakers streak
item.description: Josh Giddey continues his remarkable form but it is not enough to give the Chicago Bulls a much-needed win.

item.title: Friday Finance with David Chau
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Bulldogs 'tick off' another item on 2026 AFL season bucket list
item.description: A brilliant Oskar Baker goal clinches a thrilling win for the Bulldogs over the Crows, leaving Luke Beveridge's side unbeaten after three matches.

item.title: Southern Cloud mystery
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Alice Springs prison guards strike for 12 hours, march through streets in industrial action calling for safer conditions
item.description: Alice Springs prison guards have taken industrial action, including a 12-hour work stoppage across the town's correctional facilities, over what they say are unsafe conditions.

item.title: ABC meteorologist Tom Saunders on the forecast for Cyclone Narelle over the Northern Territory.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Chuck Norris, film star and martial artist, dies aged 86 from sudden illness
item.description: Norris' epic fight with kung-fu superstar Bruce Lee in The Way of The Dragon helped him Norris into an icon, and he went on to star in more than 20 movies.

item.title: Norway's Crown Princess Mette-Marit says she wished she never met sex offender Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: Norway's Crown Princess has told national TV that she had been "manipulated and deceived" by Jeffrey Epstein and wished she had never met him.

item.title: Politics is about to become all about gas
item.description: Scott Morrison's assertion that the chase to secure vaccines in early 2021 was "not a race" cost him dearly. A similar global contest is already underway, only this time it involves securing energy supplies.

item.title: Donald Trump calls NATO 'cowards' over Strait of Hormuz as oil prices spike
item.description: The US president claims it would be "easy" for members of the western alliance to protect the shipping route, amid reports America plans to blockade Iran's strategically-important Kharg Island.

item.title: From pub knitting to wonky ceramics, arts and crafts are having a resurgence
item.description: Craft trends come and go, but a resurgence in social arts and crafts is proving that creativity and human connection are still very much in vogue.

item.title: Greig Fraser filmed the unfilmable for Dune. Now he's back for Project Hail Mary
item.description: With most of Project Hail Mary's action taking place in far off corners of space it was up to Australian cinematographer Greig Fraser to create what we've never seen.

item.title: In the Strait of Hormuz, Iran unleashes a plot against the West years in the making
item.description: By moving so much of the war into the Strait of Hormuz and Persian Gulf, Tehran has hit its adversaries where it hurts the most and analysts warn that while the United States military is mighty, history shows in asymmetrical warfare, the lesser power can win the day.

item.title: Government leaves door open to gas, coal tax as companies warn it will threaten supply
item.description: The Greens and One Nation are among those that want gas profits levied, with pressure mounting on Labor to respond to growing calls for reform.

item.title: Coroner finds Worimi woman's death at Lightning Ridge hospital was preventable
item.description: The coroner found that a mother of three could have been saved if a transfer from a remote New South Wales hospital had been ordered sooner.

item.title: Cyclone Narelle tracker map: Where is it forecast to cross the NT?
item.description: Track the path of Tropical Cyclone Narelle and see live wind speeds as the system approaches the Northern Territory.

item.title: Peak fashion body aims to rebuild local manufacturing battered by offshore supply chain interruptions
item.description: Australia's peak clothing and textile organisation launches a national strategy to revitalise an industry hit by years of turmoil.

item.title: The government says fuel supply is safe until mid-April. What happens after that?
item.description: The government is responding to fuel shortages due to the war in the Middle East and says Australia has enough supply until mid-April. Here's what experts say could happen after that.

item.title: Live: Thousands more US Marines to be deployed to Middle East, officials say
item.description: The White House claims it could "take out" Iran's strategically important Kharg Island "any time" that Donald Trump gives the order.

item.title: Cyclone Narelle dumps hundreds of millimetres of rain, cuts power, communities on flood watch
item.description: Hundreds of millimetres of rain drench parts of Far North Queensland, with authorities urging residents to remain on flood watch as Tropical Cyclone Narelle recovery efforts begin.

item.title: Patrons of Children First Foundation condemn planned sale of donated land
item.description: A charity known for the landmark separation of conjoined twins Krishna and Trishna lists a regional Victorian property for sale against the wishes of its donor.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of 2000s hip-hop, Mercury in retrograde and disturbing kids' TV puppets
item.description: Every question has only two options: one is right and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

item.title: Australia's Cameron McEvoy breaks men's world 50 metres freestyle record
item.description: Australia's Cameron McEvoy sets a world record in the men's 50 metres freestyle, dipping under the previous mark that had stood since 2009.

item.title: The Snowy Mountains mystery of the Southern Cloud aviation disaster
item.description: When eight people vanished without a trace in 1931, Australia's first major airline disaster became a 27-year mystery that gripped the nation, until a hiker's chance discovery changed everything.

item.title: Polls predict easy Labor win in SA election, but One Nation results will be closely watched
item.description: Both polls and pundits are expecting Labor to comfortably win today's election, but exactly who Labor will be beating — and who it will face in the next parliament — is the key question.

item.title: Last Man Standing ultra running challenge pushing people to their limits
item.description: Ultra runner Tim Kacprzak started 2026 by running 1 kilometre, and has added a kilometre each day in his attempt to win the Last Man Standing challenge.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Young Japanese-Australians showing up loud and proud for final
item.description: Young Japanese diaspora in Sydney will be showing up loud and proud in support of Japan against Australia's Matildas at the Asian Cup final on Saturday.

item.title: SA One Nation volunteers accused of filling out how-to-vote cards before handing them to voters
item.description: Candidates in two separate seats in the South Australia state election complain that One Nation volunteers are writing in preferences on how-to-vote cards, despite the party's approved cards not including any preferences beyond 1 for One Nation.

item.title: Live: Rivers rise in Queensland as cyclone to pick up steam ahead severe NT impact
item.description: The category two system is expected to strengthen today as it heads towards the Northern Territory. Follow live.

item.title: Space junk is cluttering Earth's low orbit with high-velocity projectiles and it's getting worse
item.description: Thousands of satellites are being launched into space at low Earth orbit, joining hundreds of thousands of pieces of space junk, and it's becoming a real problem.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: When do the Matildas play Japan in the final? How to watch and follow the decider
item.description: The Matildas' quest for the Women's Asian Cup trophy reaches its climax tonight, when they face Japan at Sydney's Stadium Australia. Here's how to watch and follow the game.

item.title: Australia's Nicola Olyslagers wins silver at World Athletics Indoor Championships in Poland
item.description: Australia's two-time defending champion Nicola Olyslagers reaches the podium in the women's high jump at the World Athletics Indoor Championships in Poland.

item.title: New Whitsundays Ngaro Track hiking trail fuels debate over tourism in national parks
item.description: With only 15 walkers a day and costing $150 per person, the new Ngaro Track sparks debate about commercial tourism in Queensland's national parks.

item.title: Western New South Wales farmers bear brunt of fuel redistribution
item.description: Scott Worthington has run out of fuel, after first running out of water on his drought-stricken farm, and watching his pastures turn to dirt.

item.title: Why I watch Married at First Sight with my teenage son
item.description: Watching the largely fake, but also completely true, juggernaut reality TV series Married at First Sight with our 14-year-old was never a deliberate choice but it is potentially very powerful.

item.title: Quarter of Tasmanian police family violence orders issued against women, with fears victims are being misidentified
item.description: On-the-spot family violence orders are designed to protect those impacted by domestic and family violence. But advocates say they can unfavourably backfire on victim-survivors.

item.title: It's not quite a royal visit, but Tasmania has made a step to better government transparency
item.description: It was a glamorous royal visit which dominated headlines in Tasmania this week, but some other — less glitzy — developments have the potential to rebuild the public's trust in the government.

item.title: Pair arrested after trying to enter naval base hosting UK's nuclear submarines
item.description: The man and woman were arrested after trying to enter HM Naval Base Clyde on Thursday, Scottish Police say.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: How the goldfish mentality is helping Matildas defender Kaitlyn Torpey
item.description: A Ted Lasso lesson, and an Australian indie rock classic, are inspiring Kaitlyn Torpey to take her career to new heights, as she prepares for tonight's Women's Asian Cup final.

item.title: Warning system upgrades to come amid Northern Territory flood emergencies
item.description: The Northern Territory and federal governments promise to improve flood warning systems after flood-ravaged communities say they were put at risk during rushed evacuations.

item.title: Polls open for South Australia's 2026 election
item.description: South Australians are heading to the polls today for the 2026 state election where One Nation could threaten the Liberal Party's status as the official opposition and Labor is expected to have a landslide victory.
